rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Against Petitioners Sheila Siegel And Suzanne Borsuk; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Respondent Mark Borsuk's Motion for Order Compelling Responses to Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents and, and for Sanctions Against Petitioners Sheila Seigel and Suzanne Borsuk. The Motion seeks an order compelling responses from each Petitioner to: Form Interrogatory No. 4.1, 4.2, 6.1, 8.1, 9.1, 9.2, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.6, 14.1, and 14.2; Special Interrogatories, Set One, No. 93, 94, 95, 96, 97, 98, 99, 100, 101, 102, 103, 104, 107, 108, 109, 110, 111, 112, 113, 114, 115, 116, 117; and Request for Production of Documents, Set One, No. 32, 33, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 48, 50, 51. Per the Notice of Motion, the motion is made pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010 et seq and 2031.300. The Motion for Order Compelling Responses to Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents and, and for Sanctions Against Petitioners Sheila Seigel and Suzanne Borsuk is DENIED. Petitioners' Petition for Order 1. Removing Trustee and Surcharge; 2. Compelling Trustee to Account; 3. Reducing Trustee Compensation; 4. Barring Trustee's Use of Trust Funds for Litigation; and 5. Awarding Damages and Attorney's Fees and Costs for Financial Elder Abuse was filed on July 8, 2022. Respondent Mark Borsuk filed Objections to the Petition in both his individual capacity and as Trustee of the subject trust ("ALBAR Trust"), on February 24, 2023. The discovery now at issue was propounded on May 17, 2023. In the record before the court, the parties have debated what is appropriate informational "fishing" in the discovery context. Long-standing case law guides parties and the trial courts on appropriate civil discovery methods: In Greyhound Corp. v. Superior Court [ ] the seminal case in California civil discovery, the court gave examples of improper "fishing" which clearly apply here: "The method of 'fishing' may be, in a particular case, entirely improper (i.e., insufficient identification of the requested information to acquaint the other party with the nature of information desired, attempt to place the burden and cost of supplying information equally available to both solely upon the adversary, placing more burden upon the adversary than the value of the information warrants, etc.). Such improper methods of 'fishing' may be (and should be) controlled by the trial court under the powers granted to it by the statute." Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 216, 225, quoting Greyhound Corp. v. Superior Court (1961) 56 Cal.2d 355. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Against Petitioners Sheila Siegel And Suzanne Borsuk; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Added To Calendar For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Other contested Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents seek communications related to distributions to either Petitioner from the ALBAR Trust (e.g., Special Rog. No. 107), and communications between Respondent and Petitioners about the ALBAR Trust or about the Petition (e.g., Request for Production No. 44, 45, 46). Based on the substantive exhibits Respondent has filed with the court as part of the Objection to the underlying Petition, and his full-time role as trustee/asset manager of the Trust's real estate investment business, the court concludes Respondent would have at least equal access to the information and materials sought in these disputed discovery requests (see, Code of Civ. Proc. section 2030.220(c)). Within the context of the financial elder abuse claims, the Petition does allege an unspecified harm to Petitioner Seigel caused by the tax liability on her beneficial interest being greater than the distribution she received (e.g., Pet. at 23:21-22). These allegations of harm are vague, parenthetical, and limited to Seigel's personal tax liability. Nevertheless, contested Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents seek "all" personal and business tax documents and information, from both Petitioners, for years beyond the alleged period of harm. On this record, the court does not find that the information or materials sought in these contested Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents are within the scope of permissible discovery or reasonably calculated to lead to discoverable information (see Code of Civ. Proc. section 2017.010; see also Code of Civ. Proc. section 2023.010(a), misuse of discovery includes "Persisting, over objection and without substantial justification, in an attempt to obtain information or materials that are outside the scope of permissible discovery."). Petitioners' request for sanctions is GRANTED. Sanctions are awarded pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2030.300(d), 2023.010(a), 2023.010(h), and 2031.310(h). The Court finds that it would not be unjust to impose sanctions on this unsuccessful motion. Good cause having been shown, Respondent is ordered to pay Petitioners sanctions on this motion in the amount of seven thousand dollars ($7,000), within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. (See, Karlsson v. Ford Motor Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1202, 1217 ["Discovery sanctions must be tailored in order to remedy the offending party's discovery abuse, should not give the aggrieved party more than what it is entitled to, and should not be used to punish the offending party."].) Petitioners shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions Against Petitioners Sheila Siegel And Suzanne Borsuk; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Added To Calendar For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] The moving and opposing papers show Petitioners responded to the contested discovery with substantive objections. Service of these objections was followed by substantive albeit unsuccessful meet and confer efforts by counsel for the parties, which included service of further written responses to each form of discovery (See, Decl. of Krasner and Exhs 2-7; Decl. of Clark and Exhs. A-D). The court does not find that either Petitioner "fail[ed] to serve a timely response to [a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling]," pursuant to Civ. Proc. Code section 2031.300. Similarly, on the record presented on this motion, the court does not find that either Petitioner "misuse[d] the discovery process" pursuant to Civ. Proc. Code section 2023.010, by, for example, "Failing to respond or to submit to an authorized method of discovery." (sub. d); "Making, without substantial justification, an unmeritorious objection to discovery." (subd. e); or "Making an evasive response to discovery." (subd. f). As Respondent summarizes in the Objection to Petition, he has been in the "role as trustee/asset manager [of the ALBAR Trust, and] as a leasing broker for running [the parties'] real estate investment business on a full-time basis" for several years. (Obj. to Pet. at 10:16-17.) The Petition alleges breach of the Trustee's fiduciary duty, and further alleges related claims of alleged financial elder abuse of the beneficiary-Petitioners by the Trustee, by withholding appropriate distributions. These claims arise from an alleged course of conduct by Respondent spanning several years, and not from a single "incident." The contested Form Interrogatories that seek information regarding "the incident" as the term is defined under Form Interrogatory DISC-0001 are untenably vague in this context. (See also, Civ. Proc. C. section 2033.710, form interrogatories were developed for "use in any civil action in a state court based on personal injury, property damage, wrongful death, unlawful detainer, breach of contract, family law, or fraud and for any other civil actions the Judicial Council deems appropriate.") Respondent's motion to compel responses to the contested Form Interrogatories, beyond the Petitioners' existing responses and further responses to the contested Form Interrogatories, is denied. The Petitioner's claims arise from Respondent's actions as a Trustee of the ALBAR Trust. The claims of financial elder abuse are based on allegations that the Trustee wrongly and intentionally withheld distributions from the ALBAR Trust due to Petitioners, to their detriment (Pet. at 23:10-26:26). On the record before the court, the contested Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents that seek information or materials relating to non-Trust assets held by either Petitioner or non-Trust distributions to either Petitioner or to either Petitioner's personal financial or business information are not within the scope of permissible discovery or reasonably calculated to lead to discoverable information (see Code of Civ. Proc. section 2017.010; see also Code of Civ. Proc. section 2023.010(a), misuse of discovery includes "Persisting, over objection and without substantial justification, in an attempt to obtain information or materials that are outside the scope of permissible discovery."). [Part 2 of 3]
